section_miscategorizations.reason: Although phrased as a patient report, absence of tenderness is typically a physical exam finding; however, it could plausibly be patient-reported, so this is a minor issue.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a one plus posterior drawer and negative anterior drawer.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Posterior and anterior drawer tests are clinician-performed exam maneuvers; documenting them as patient-reported is misleading about the source of the finding.
Joint laxity is an exam finding; attributing it to patient report in the Objective section obscures that it should be a clinician-observed physical exam result.
Abrasions are observable physical findings; phrasing as patient report in the Objective section misrepresents the nature/source of the observation.
Joint clicking is typically a symptom the patient experiences; as written, it is a patient-reported symptom and belongs in Subjective, not Objective.
Tenderness here is framed as patient-reported rather than elicited on exam; this makes it a symptom and thus subjective.
Pain intensity is a patient-reported symptom and should be in the Subjective section, not Objective.
This is a symptom statement, not an assessment or diagnostic impression; it belongs in the Subjective history of present illness.
Hand pain is a patient-reported symptom and should be documented in the Subjective section, not in the Assessment.
This describes prior/current medication use, not a new order or change; it belongs in the Subjective medication history.
